heading: Location of foreign missions in the District of Columbia
label: 4306 Location of foreign missions in the District of Columbia
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 1, 1982section 204 of Pub. L. 97–241section 4301 of this title

children.heading: Section as governing location, replacement, or expansion
children.label: a Section as governing location, replacement, or expansion
children.text: The location, replacement, or expansion of chanceries in the District of Columbia shall be subject to this section.

children.heading: Acceptable areas; limitations and conditions
children.label: b Acceptable areas; limitations and conditions

children.text: A chancery shall be permitted to locate as a matter of right in any area which is zoned commercial, industrial, waterfront, or mixed-use (CR).

children.label: A
children.text: in any area which is zoned medium-high or high density residential, and

children.label: B
children.text: in any other area, determined on the basis of existing uses, which includes office or institutional uses, including but not limited to any area zoned mixed-use diplomatic or special purpose,
In each of the areas described in paragraphs (1) and (2), the limitations and conditions applicable to chanceries shall not exceed those applicable to other office or institutional uses in that area.

children.heading: Filing of application with Board of Zoning Adjustment; publication of notice; public participation; final determination
children.label: c Filing of application with Board of Zoning Adjustment; publication of notice; public participation; final determination

children.text: If a foreign mission wishes to locate a chancery in an area described in subsection (b)(2), or wishes to appeal an administrative decision relating to a chancery based in whole or in part upon any zoning map or regulation, it shall file an application with the Board of Zoning Adjustment which shall publish notice of that application in the District of Columbia Register.
Regulations issued to carry out this section shall provide appropriate opportunities for participation by the public in proceedings concerning the location, replacement, or expansion of chanceries.

A final determination concerning the location, replacement, or expansion of a chancery shall be made not later than six months after the date of the filing of an application with respect to such location, replacement, or expansion. Such determination shall not be subject to the administrative proceedings of any other agency or official except as provided in this chapter.
The international obligation of the United States to facilitate the provision of adequate and secure facilities for foreign missions in the Nation’s Capital.

Historic preservation, as determined by the Board of Zoning Adjustment in carrying out this section; and in order to ensure compatibility with historic landmarks and districts, substantial compliance with District of Columbia and Federal regulations governing historic preservation shall be required with respect to new construction and to demolition of or alteration to historic landmarks.

The adequacy of off-street or other parking and the extent to which the area will be served by public transportation to reduce parking requirements, subject to such special security requirements as may be determined by the Secretary, after consultation with Federal agencies authorized to perform protective services.
The extent to which the area is capable of being adequately protected, as determined by the Secretary, after consultation with Federal agencies authorized to perform protective services.
The municipal interest, as determined by the Mayor of the District of Columbia.
The Federal interest, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Consistency of regulations, proceedings, and other actions; review and comment by National Planning Commission
children.label: e Consistency of regulations, proceedings, and other actions; review and comment by National Planning Commission

children.text: Regulations, proceedings, and other actions of the National Capital Planning Commission, the Zoning Commission for the District of Columbia, and the Board of Zoning Adjustment affecting the location, replacement, or expansion of chanceries shall be consistent with this section (including the criteria set out in subsection (d)) and shall reflect the policy of this chapter.
Proposed actions of the Zoning Commission concerning implementation of this section shall be referred to the National Capital Planning Commission for review and comment.

children.heading: Rule-making nature of proceedings
children.label: f Rule-making nature of proceedings
children.text: Regulations issued to carry out this section shall provide for proceedings of a rule-making and not of an adjudicatory nature.

children.heading: Compliance with District of Columbia building and related codes
children.label: g Compliance with District of Columbia building and related codes
children.text: The Secretary shall require foreign missions to comply substantially with District of Columbia building and related codes in a manner determined by the Secretary to be not inconsistent with the international obligations of the United States.

children.heading: Approval of Board of Zoning Adjustment or Zoning Commission not required
children.label: h Approval of Board of Zoning Adjustment or Zoning Commission not required

children.text: for the location, replacement, or expansion of a chancery to the extent that authority to proceed, or rights or interests, with respect to such location, replacement, or expansion were granted to or otherwise acquired by the foreign mission before ; orOctober 1, 1982
for continuing use of a chancery by a foreign mission to the extent that the chancery was being used by a foreign mission on .October 1, 1982

children.heading: Membership on Zoning Commission and Board of Zoning Adjustment
children.label: i Membership on Zoning Commission and Board of Zoning Adjustment

children.text: The President may designate the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of the Interior, or the Administrator of General Services (or such alternate as such official may from time to time designate) to serve as a member of the Zoning Commission in lieu of the Director of the National Park Service whenever the President determines that the Zoning Commission is performing functions concerning the implementation of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the representative from the Zoning Commission shall be the Director of the National Park Service or if another person has been designated under paragraph (1) of this subsection, the person so designated; and

children.label: B
children.text: the representative from the National Capital Planning Commission shall be the Executive Director of that Commission.

children.heading: Application of other laws
children.label: j Application of other laws
children.text: Provisions of law (other than this chapter) applicable with respect to the location, replacement, or expansion of real property in the District of Columbia shall apply with respect to chanceries only to the extent that they are consistent with this section.
